events.eventText: First ancient Olympic Games held in Greece
events.description: Only event was a 192m footrace called the stadion

events.eventText: Julius Caesar is assassinated in Rome
events.description: Stabbed 23 times on the Ides of March

events.eventText: Gutenberg prints the first Bible
events.description: Movable type launched a revolution in knowledge

events.eventText: Magellan's crew completes first circumnavigation
events.description: Magellan himself was killed in the Philippines

events.eventText: Isaac Newton publishes Principia Mathematica
events.description: Laid the foundation for classical mechanics

events.eventText: The Bastille is stormed in Paris
events.description: Became the defining symbol of the French Revolution

events.eventText: Darwin publishes On the Origin of Species
events.description: Sold out its first print run in a single day

events.eventText: The Titanic sinks on its maiden voyage
events.description: Over 1,500 died in the freezing North Atlantic

events.eventText: Neil Armstrong walks on the Moon
events.description: The mission computer had less power than a calculator

events.eventText: The Berlin Wall falls
events.description: East Germans flooded through after a confused press conference

events.eventText: Alhazen completes the Book of Optics
events.description: Founded the scientific method through experiments with light

events.eventText: Constantinople falls to the Ottomans
events.description: Ended over 1,000 years of the Byzantine Empire

events.eventText: Martin Luther nails the 95 Theses to a church door
events.description: Sparked the Protestant Reformation across Europe

events.eventText: The Spanish Armada is defeated
events.description: Storms did as much damage as the English fleet

events.eventText: Shakespeare writes Hamlet
events.description: The longest of his plays at over 4,000 lines

events.eventText: Galileo observes Jupiter's moons
events.description: Proved not everything orbits the Earth

events.eventText: The King James Bible is published
events.description: Took 47 scholars seven years to complete

events.eventText: The Taj Mahal is completed in Agra
events.description: Built by 20,000 workers as a memorial to a beloved wife

events.eventText: Alexander Graham Bell patents the telephone
events.description: Beat a rival application by just two hours

events.eventText: Spotify launches in Sweden
events.description: Now has over 600 million users worldwide

events.eventText: Black Death arrives in Sicily via trading ships
events.description: Killed roughly a third of Europe's population within 5 years

events.eventText: The US Declaration of Independence is signed
events.description: Most delegates actually signed it on 2 August

events.eventText: Edward Jenner tests the smallpox vaccine
events.description: Used cowpox pus from a milkmaid's hand

events.eventText: France adopts the metric system
events.description: Replaced hundreds of local measurement systems

events.eventText: Haitian slaves defeat Napoleon's army
events.description: Led to the only successful large-scale slave revolt

events.eventText: Napoleon is defeated at Waterloo
events.description: His final battle lasted less than a day

events.eventText: Samuel Morse sends the first telegraph message
events.description: Sent from Washington to Baltimore

events.eventText: Japan attacks Pearl Harbor
events.description: Brought the United States into World War II

events.eventText: The WHO declares smallpox eradicated
events.description: The only human disease ever completely wiped out

events.eventText: Lehman Brothers collapses
events.description: Largest bankruptcy in US history at the time

events.eventText: The first coins are minted in Lydia
events.description: Made from a gold-silver alloy called electrum

events.eventText: Siddhartha Gautama is born in Lumbini
events.description: Founder of Buddhism, raised as a prince in Nepal

events.eventText: Athenian democracy is established by Cleisthenes
events.description: Citizens voted directly on laws — no representatives

events.eventText: Battle of Marathon is fought
events.description: A Greek runner allegedly died delivering news of the victory

events.eventText: The Parthenon is completed in Athens
events.description: Built without mortar — precision-cut marble held it together

events.eventText: Alexander the Great founds Alexandria
events.description: Named at least 20 cities after himself

events.eventText: Vesuvius erupts and buries Pompeii
events.description: Preserved the city so well that bread was found in ovens

events.eventText: Charlemagne is crowned Holy Roman Emperor
events.description: United most of Western Europe for the first time since Rome

events.eventText: Rosa Parks refuses to give up her bus seat
events.description: Sparked the 381-day Montgomery bus boycott

events.eventText: The first mRNA COVID-19 vaccine is administered
events.description: Developed in under a year — a record for any vaccine

events.eventText: The Colosseum opens in Rome
events.description: Could seat 50,000 and had a retractable awning

events.eventText: Genghis Khan is proclaimed ruler of all Mongols
events.description: Went on to build the largest contiguous land empire

events.eventText: The Eiffel Tower opens for the Paris World Fair
events.description: Originally planned to be dismantled after 20 years

events.eventText: Wright Brothers fly at Kitty Hawk
events.description: The first powered flight lasted just 12 seconds

events.eventText: The Battle of the Somme begins
events.description: Nearly 20,000 British soldiers died on the first day alone

events.eventText: Alexander Fleming discovers penicillin
events.description: Found by accident when mould contaminated a petri dish

events.eventText: The Wall Street Crash triggers panic selling
events.description: The Dow didn't recover its pre-crash high until 1954

events.eventText: D-Day landings at Normandy
events.description: Over 150,000 Allied troops crossed the English Channel

events.eventText: Martin Luther King delivers the I Have a Dream speech
events.description: The most famous part was improvised on the spot

events.eventText: Nelson Mandela is elected President of South Africa
events.description: Voted in the country's first fully democratic election

events.eventText: The Magna Carta is sealed at Runnymede
events.description: Forced King John to accept limits on royal power

events.eventText: Columbus reaches the Caribbean
events.description: Believed he had reached Asia until his death

events.eventText: Napoleon crowns himself Emperor of France
events.description: Took the crown from the Pope and placed it on his own head

events.eventText: The Suez Canal opens to shipping
events.description: Cut the London-to-Mumbai sea route by 7,000 km

events.eventText: Marie Curie isolates radium
events.description: Her notebooks are still too radioactive to handle

events.eventText: The Russian Revolution — Tsar Nicholas II abdicates
events.description: Abdicated in a railway carriage near Pskov

events.eventText: Amelia Earhart flies solo across the Atlantic
events.description: Completed the crossing in under 15 hours

events.eventText: Picasso paints Guernica
events.description: Created in response to the bombing of a Basque town

events.eventText: Watson and Crick describe DNA's double helix
events.description: Built on crucial X-ray work by Rosalind Franklin

events.eventText: Bouazizi's protest sparks the Arab Spring
events.description: Triggered uprisings across the Middle East

events.eventText: Hammurabi's Code is inscribed in Babylon
events.description: One of the earliest written legal codes with 282 laws

events.eventText: Cai Lun presents paper to the Chinese emperor
events.description: Replaced bamboo strips and silk as a writing surface

events.eventText: The Printing Revolution begins in Europe
events.description: Within 50 years, 20 million volumes had been printed

events.eventText: Robert Hooke publishes Micrographia
events.description: First detailed illustrations of life under a microscope

events.eventText: Captain Cook lands at Botany Bay
events.description: Aboriginal Australians had lived there for 65,000 years

events.eventText: Louis Pasteur demonstrates pasteurisation
events.description: Originally designed to save the French wine industry

events.eventText: Thomas Edison opens the first power station
events.description: Lit up a square mile of lower Manhattan

events.eventText: The Panama Canal opens to shipping
events.description: Cut the New York-to-San Francisco journey by 12,000 km

events.eventText: Yuri Gagarin orbits the Earth
events.description: His entire flight lasted just 108 minutes

events.eventText: Tim Berners-Lee launches the first website
events.description: The page explained what the World Wide Web was for

events.eventText: Marco Polo arrives at Kublai Khan's court
events.description: Spent 17 years in the Mongol Empire

events.eventText: Cortés captures Tenochtitlan from the Aztecs
events.description: Smallpox killed far more Aztecs than Spanish weapons

events.eventText: Beethoven premieres his Ninth Symphony
events.description: Conducted while almost completely deaf

events.eventText: Britain's Slavery Abolition Act takes effect
events.description: Freed over 800,000 enslaved people

events.eventText: The Irish potato blight is first reported
events.description: Ireland's population has still never recovered

events.eventText: Gregor Mendel publishes his laws of inheritance
events.description: Experimented with 28,000 pea plants in a monastery garden

events.eventText: The Statue of Liberty is unveiled in New York
events.description: Arrived from France in 350 pieces packed in 214 crates

events.eventText: Karl Benz patents the first petrol automobile
events.description: Had three wheels and a top speed of 16 km/h

events.eventText: The Soviet Union launches Sputnik
events.description: Its beeps could be picked up by amateur radio worldwide

events.eventText: The WHO declares COVID-19 a global pandemic
events.description: Led to the fastest vaccine development in history

events.eventText: The Library of Alexandria is founded
events.description: Aimed to contain a copy of every book in the world

events.eventText: Muhammad arrives in Medina (the Hijra)
events.description: Year one of the Islamic calendar

events.eventText: The East India Company receives its royal charter
events.description: Became the most powerful corporation in history

events.eventText: Lumière brothers screen the first film
events.description: Audiences reportedly fled from an oncoming train

events.eventText: The Republic of China is proclaimed
events.description: Ended 2,000 years of imperial rule

events.eventText: Spanish Flu hits Camp Funston in Kansas
events.description: The pandemic went on to kill 50-100 million worldwide

events.eventText: Gandhi sets off on the Salt March
events.description: Walked 390 km to protest the British salt monopoly

events.eventText: Mao Zedong proclaims the People's Republic of China
events.description: Declared from the Gate of Heavenly Peace in Beijing

events.eventText: The Beatles appear on The Ed Sullivan Show
events.description: Watched by 73 million Americans — a record at the time

events.eventText: Facebook launches from a Harvard dorm room
events.description: Had one million users within its first year

events.eventText: Cyrus the Great conquers Babylon
events.description: Created the first charter of human rights

events.eventText: Leif Erikson lands in North America
events.description: Beat Columbus by nearly 500 years

events.eventText: The Aztec capital Tenochtitlan is founded
events.description: Built on a lake island where Mexico City now stands

events.eventText: Vasco da Gama reaches India by sea
events.description: Opened the spice trade route around Africa

events.eventText: The Treaty of Westphalia is signed
events.description: Established the modern concept of state sovereignty

events.eventText: The US Constitution is ratified
events.description: Oldest written national constitution still in use

events.eventText: Simón Bolívar liberates Venezuela from Spain
events.description: Known as El Libertador across South America

events.eventText: The Berlin Conference partitions Africa
events.description: Not a single African leader was invited

events.eventText: Indian independence is declared at midnight
events.description: Midnight transfer of power to avoid an astrologically bad date

events.eventText: The September 11 attacks strike New York
events.description: Nearly 3,000 people killed in the deadliest terror attack in history

events.eventText: The Han Dynasty is established in China
events.description: Gave its name to China's largest ethnic group

events.eventText: Dante completes The Divine Comedy
events.description: Wrote in Italian instead of Latin, shocking the literary world

events.eventText: Leonardo da Vinci begins the Mona Lisa
events.description: Carried it with him for years, endlessly refining it

events.eventText: Copernicus publishes De revolutionibus
events.description: Published on his deathbed to avoid controversy

events.eventText: The Dutch East India Company issues shares
events.description: The first company to sell public stock

events.eventText: Voltaire publishes Candide
events.description: A savage satire on optimism, banned across Europe

events.eventText: James Watt patents his improved steam engine
events.description: Made steam power efficient enough for factories

events.eventText: Napoleonic Code is enacted
events.description: Influenced legal systems across Europe and Latin America

events.eventText: Reactor 4 at Chernobyl explodes
events.description: Created a 2,600 km² exclusion zone that still exists

events.eventText: ChatGPT launches and triggers the AI boom
events.description: Reached 100 million users faster than any app in history

events.eventText: Emperor Qin Shi Huang unifies China
events.description: Ordered construction of the first Great Wall sections

events.eventText: The Kingdom of Aksum adopts Christianity
events.description: One of the first states in the world to do so

events.eventText: The Mughal emperor Babur captures Delhi
events.description: A descendant of both Genghis Khan and Tamerlane

events.eventText: Galileo demonstrates his telescope in Venice
events.description: Could magnify objects roughly 8 times

events.eventText: William Harvey describes blood circulation
events.description: Proved blood is pumped by the heart in a closed loop

events.eventText: William Herschel discovers Uranus
events.description: Initially thought it was a comet

events.eventText: Queen Victoria is proclaimed Empress of India
events.description: The British Empire then covered a quarter of the globe

events.eventText: ENIAC, the first electronic computer, is switched on
events.description: Weighed 30 tonnes and filled an entire room

events.eventText: Dolly the sheep is born via cloning
events.description: Named after Dolly Parton

events.eventText: The Higgs boson is detected at CERN
events.description: Confirmed a theory proposed nearly 50 years earlier

events.eventText: Hannibal crosses the Alps with war elephants
events.description: Lost nearly half his army to the mountain crossing

events.eventText: Chinese alchemists first record a gunpowder formula
events.description: Created while searching for an elixir of immortality

events.eventText: William the Conqueror wins at Hastings
events.description: Harold II reportedly killed by an arrow to the eye

events.eventText: The Medici Bank opens in Florence
events.description: Financed the Renaissance through banking innovation

events.eventText: Vesalius publishes his anatomy textbook
events.description: Corrected centuries of anatomical errors from Galen

events.eventText: The Great Fire of London breaks out
events.description: Destroyed 13,200 houses but officially killed only six

events.eventText: The Battle of Gettysburg is fought
events.description: Turning point of the American Civil War with 50,000 casualties

events.eventText: Marconi transmits a radio signal across the Atlantic
events.description: Sent the letter S in Morse code from Cornwall to Newfoundland

events.eventText: The Cuban Missile Crisis brings the world to the brink
events.description: For 13 days, nuclear war seemed imminent

events.eventText: Steve Jobs unveils the iPhone
events.description: Introduced it as three products in one

events.eventText: Construction of Angkor Wat begins
events.description: Largest religious monument ever built

events.eventText: Mansa Musa arrives in Cairo on his pilgrimage
events.description: Gave away so much gold he crashed Egypt's economy for a decade

events.eventText: The Mayflower lands at Plymouth
events.description: Only about half the passengers survived the first winter

events.eventText: A massive earthquake destroys Lisbon
events.description: Triggered a tsunami and fires that levelled the city

events.eventText: The US drops an atomic bomb on Hiroshima
events.description: The bomber crew saw the flash from 600 km away

events.eventText: The Partition of India creates Pakistan
events.description: Triggered one of the largest mass migrations in history

events.eventText: The NHS opens in Britain
events.description: Free healthcare for all, funded by taxation

events.eventText: George Orwell publishes Nineteen Eighty-Four
events.description: Coined terms like Big Brother and thoughtcrime

events.eventText: Fidel Castro enters Havana
events.description: Was just 32 years old when he seized power

events.eventText: Argentina wins the 2022 World Cup in Qatar
events.description: Messi's last World Cup, decided on penalties

events.eventText: Confucius begins teaching in the state of Lu
events.description: His ideas shaped East Asian culture for 2,500 years

events.eventText: Jesus of Nazareth is crucified in Jerusalem
events.description: Launched what became the world's largest religion

events.eventText: The House of Wisdom is founded in Baghdad
events.description: Translated and preserved Greek, Persian, and Indian texts

events.eventText: Admiral Zheng He departs on his first great voyage
events.description: His fleet of 300 ships dwarfed anything in Europe

events.eventText: The South Sea Bubble bursts
events.description: Even Isaac Newton lost a fortune in the crash

events.eventText: Mozart composes his first symphony aged eight
events.description: Went on to write over 600 works before dying at 35

events.eventText: France adopts the Declaration of the Rights of Man
events.description: Declared that men are born and remain free and equal

events.eventText: Florence Nightingale arrives at Scutari hospital
events.description: Reduced the death rate from 42% to 2%

events.eventText: The first modern Olympic Games open in Athens
events.description: Just 241 athletes from 14 nations competed

events.eventText: Perseverance rover lands on Mars
events.description: Carried a helicopter that flew on another planet

events.eventText: The Rosetta Stone is carved in Egypt
events.description: Written in three scripts, it became the key to hieroglyphs

events.eventText: Paper-making arrives in Samarkand from China
events.description: Captured Chinese craftsmen taught the technique

events.eventText: The first printed newspaper is published in Strasbourg
events.description: Called Relation, it covered news from across Europe

events.eventText: The Liverpool-Manchester Railway opens
events.description: A politician was killed by a train at the opening ceremony

events.eventText: Thomas Edison patents the phonograph
events.description: The first recording was Mary Had a Little Lamb

events.eventText: Wilhelm Röntgen takes the first X-ray image
events.description: X-rayed his wife's hand — she said she had seen her death

events.eventText: The BBC launches the first regular TV broadcasts
events.description: Transmitted from Alexandra Palace in London

events.eventText: Martin Cooper makes the first mobile phone call
events.description: Called his rival at Bell Labs to gloat

events.eventText: The first text message is sent in the UK
events.description: Neil Papworth texted Merry Christmas from a computer

events.eventText: Bitcoin is launched by Satoshi Nakamoto
events.description: The first decentralised cryptocurrency

events.eventText: Cyrus the Great issues the Cyrus Cylinder
events.description: Sometimes called the first declaration of human rights

events.eventText: The Twelve Tables of Roman law are published
events.description: Displayed publicly so all citizens could read them

events.eventText: Hagia Sophia is completed in Constantinople
events.description: Its dome was the world's largest for nearly 1,000 years

events.eventText: The English Bill of Rights is enacted
events.description: Established parliamentary supremacy over the crown

events.eventText: New Zealand grants women the right to vote
events.description: First country to do so, after a 32,000-signature petition

events.eventText: The Empire State Building is completed
events.description: Built in just 410 days during the Great Depression

events.eventText: The UN adopts the Universal Declaration of Human Rights
events.description: Drafted by a committee chaired by Eleanor Roosevelt

events.eventText: The US Civil Rights Act is signed into law
events.description: Outlawed discrimination based on race, colour, religion, or sex

events.eventText: Ireland legalises same-sex marriage by vote
events.description: First country to do so by popular vote

events.eventText: The Paris Climate Agreement is signed
events.description: 196 nations agreed to limit global warming

events.eventText: The Plague of Athens breaks out
events.description: Killed up to a quarter of the Athenian army

events.eventText: The Pantheon is completed in Rome
events.description: Its unreinforced concrete dome remains the world's largest

events.eventText: Gutenberg builds his printing press
events.description: Reduced the cost of books by roughly 80%

events.eventText: James Lind proves citrus prevents scurvy
events.description: The Royal Navy didn't adopt his advice for 42 years

events.eventText: First hot air balloon flight in Paris
events.description: The passengers were a sheep, a duck, and a rooster

events.eventText: Robert Goddard launches the first liquid-fuelled rocket
events.description: The rocket flew for just 2.5 seconds

events.eventText: Turing's team cracks the Enigma code
events.description: Estimated to have shortened the war by two years

events.eventText: Apollo 11 lands on the Moon
events.description: Armstrong and Aldrin spent 2.5 hours on the surface

events.eventText: A tsunami devastates the Indian Ocean coast
events.description: Waves reached 30 metres high and hit 14 countries

events.eventText: The Fukushima Daiichi reactor melts down
events.description: Triggered by a magnitude 9.0 earthquake and tsunami

events.eventText: Cleopatra becomes co-ruler of Egypt
events.description: Spoke nine languages and was first Ptolemy to learn Egyptian

events.eventText: Wu Zetian declares herself Empress of China
events.description: Only woman to hold the title of Empress regnant

events.eventText: Joan of Arc lifts the Siege of Orléans
events.description: Was just 17 years old when she turned the tide of the war

events.eventText: The Meiji Emperor takes the throne in Japan
events.description: Transformed Japan from feudal state to industrial power

events.eventText: Marie Curie wins her first Nobel Prize
events.description: First woman to win a Nobel, and later first person to win two

events.eventText: Valentina Tereshkova orbits the Earth
events.description: First woman in space, completing 48 orbits in 3 days

events.eventText: OPEC announces an oil embargo
events.description: Quadrupled oil prices and caused a global energy crisis

events.eventText: Nelson Mandela is released from prison
events.description: Served 27 years, mostly on Robben Island

events.eventText: Malala Yousafzai wins the Nobel Peace Prize
events.description: At 17, the youngest Nobel laureate in history

events.eventText: BTS tops the US Billboard 200 chart
events.description: Sang primarily in Korean

events.eventText: The Kingdom of Kush conquers Egypt
events.description: Nubian pharaohs ruled Egypt for nearly a century

events.eventText: Sun Tzu writes The Art of War
events.description: Still studied at military academies and business schools

events.eventText: Coffee is first brewed in Ethiopia
events.description: Legend says a goatherd noticed his goats dancing after eating berries

events.eventText: Great Zimbabwe is at the height of its power
events.description: Stone walls without mortar housed up to 18,000 people

events.eventText: Niépce captures the first photograph
events.description: Required an eight-hour exposure to capture

events.eventText: Bretton Woods Agreement is signed
events.description: 44 nations pegged their currencies to the US dollar

events.eventText: Japan surrenders, ending World War II
events.description: Emperor Hirohito broadcast the surrender by radio

events.eventText: Ghana gains independence from Britain
events.description: Led by Kwame Nkrumah, it inspired movements across Africa

events.eventText: Tiananmen Square protests are suppressed
events.description: The iconic Tank Man photo was seen worldwide

events.eventText: Barack Obama is inaugurated as US President
events.description: First African American to hold the office

events.eventText: The Epic of Gilgamesh is written in Mesopotamia
events.description: The oldest known work of literature

events.eventText: The Tang Dynasty is established in China
events.description: Poetry, art, and Silk Road trade flourished

events.eventText: Crusaders capture Jerusalem
events.description: The First Crusade ended with a brutal sack of the city

events.eventText: Ali defeats Foreman in the Rumble in the Jungle
events.description: Used the rope-a-dope strategy in Kinshasa

events.eventText: Saigon falls to North Vietnamese forces
events.description: Helicopters evacuated people from the US Embassy roof

events.eventText: Concorde makes its first commercial flight
events.description: Crossed the Atlantic in under 3.5 hours

events.eventText: Deng Xiaoping launches Chinese economic reforms
events.description: Lifted more people out of poverty than any policy in history

events.eventText: Margaret Thatcher becomes UK Prime Minister
events.description: First woman to hold the office, served 11 years

events.eventText: The first cases of HIV/AIDS are clinically identified
events.description: Has since killed over 40 million people globally

events.eventText: The African Union is established
events.description: Replaced the Organisation of African Unity

events.eventText: The Nabataeans carve Petra from sandstone cliffs
events.description: A city hidden in a desert canyon in modern Jordan

events.eventText: The Plague of Justinian strikes Constantinople
events.description: First known pandemic of bubonic plague

events.eventText: Walsingham uncovers the Babington Plot
events.description: Intercepted coded letters leading to Mary Queen of Scots' execution

events.eventText: The first opera, Dafne, is performed in Florence
events.description: Most of the music has since been lost

events.eventText: Frederick the Great orders Prussians to grow potatoes
events.description: Had to post guards on royal potato fields to create demand

events.eventText: Wollstonecraft publishes Rights of Woman
events.description: Argued women were not naturally inferior, just less educated

events.eventText: The Zulu Kingdom is established under Shaka
events.description: Revolutionised warfare with the short stabbing spear

events.eventText: Brazil abolishes slavery
events.description: Freed over 700,000 enslaved people — last in the Western Hemisphere

events.eventText: Roger Bannister runs the first sub-four-minute mile
events.description: His record lasted just 46 days before being broken

events.eventText: Usain Bolt sets the 100m world record at 9.58s
events.description: A record that still stands

events.eventText: The Inca Empire reaches its peak
events.description: Connected by 40,000 km of roads without using the wheel

events.eventText: Alcock and Brown fly nonstop across the Atlantic
events.description: They crash-landed in an Irish bog

events.eventText: The first football World Cup is held in Uruguay
events.description: Only 13 teams entered — several Europeans refused to travel

events.eventText: Jesse Owens wins four golds at the Berlin Olympics
events.description: Defied Hitler's vision of Aryan supremacy

events.eventText: The Hindenburg airship catches fire
events.description: Crashed in 37 seconds, ending the era of passenger airships

events.eventText: The Chicago Pile-1 reactor achieves a chain reaction
events.description: Built under the stands of a university football stadium

events.eventText: The Battle of Stalingrad ends
events.description: The deadliest battle in history with 2 million casualties

events.eventText: The Korean War begins
events.description: The peninsula remains divided to this day

events.eventText: The Hubble Space Telescope is launched
events.description: Its main mirror was initially ground to the wrong shape

events.eventText: Chile's miners are rescued after 69 days underground
events.description: All 33 miners survived the longest underground entrapment

events.eventText: Hippocrates establishes a medical school on Kos
events.description: Separated medicine from superstition in ancient Greece

events.eventText: The Maurya Empire unifies most of India
events.description: Emperor Ashoka later renounced violence and embraced Buddhism

events.eventText: Rome defeats Hannibal at the Battle of Zama
events.description: Ended the Second Punic War and Carthage's threat to Rome

events.eventText: Rome defeats Carthage in the Punic Wars
events.description: Carthage was razed to the ground

events.eventText: Julius Caesar uses a substitution cipher
events.description: Shifted each letter three places in the alphabet

events.eventText: Julius Caesar crosses the Rubicon river
events.description: Triggered a civil war with the words the die is cast

events.eventText: Baghdad's first public hospital opens
events.description: Had separate wards for different diseases

events.eventText: The Mongols fail to invade Japan (typhoon)
events.description: A typhoon destroyed the fleet — later called kamikaze

events.eventText: Norman Borlaug releases high-yield wheat varieties
events.description: His Green Revolution is credited with saving a billion lives

events.eventText: The International Space Station receives its first crew
events.description: Has been continuously occupied ever since

events.eventText: The Romans complete the Appian Way
events.description: Sections of the original road are still walkable today

events.eventText: Thomas Aquinas completes Summa Theologica
events.description: Attempted to reconcile faith with Aristotelian reason

events.eventText: Bartolomeu Dias rounds the Cape of Good Hope
events.description: First European to sail around the southern tip of Africa

events.eventText: The Columbian Exchange begins
events.description: Tomatoes, potatoes, and chocolate all crossed the Atlantic

events.eventText: Nicolas Appert invents canned food preservation
events.description: Developed to feed Napoleon's army on long campaigns

events.eventText: Edwin Drake drills the first oil well in Pennsylvania
events.description: Launched the petroleum age that still dominates today

events.eventText: The first genetically modified food goes on sale
events.description: The Flavr Savr tomato lasted longer on shelves

events.eventText: The Kyoto Protocol is adopted
events.description: First binding international treaty to reduce greenhouse gases

events.eventText: The first lab-grown burger is eaten in London
events.description: Cost £215,000 to produce a single patty

events.eventText: The James Webb Space Telescope launches
events.description: Can see galaxies formed just after the Big Bang

events.eventText: Stonehenge is constructed
events.description: Some stones were transported 240 km from Wales

events.eventText: Chinese astronomers record a supernova visible by day
events.description: The remnant is now the Crab Nebula

events.eventText: The Leaning Tower of Pisa begins to tilt
events.description: Started leaning during construction due to soft ground

events.eventText: Oil is discovered in Saudi Arabia
events.description: Transformed one of the poorest kingdoms into the richest

events.eventText: NATO is established by 12 Western nations
events.description: Created a mutual defence pact against Soviet expansion

events.eventText: The Berlin Wall is erected overnight
events.description: Families woke up separated by barbed wire

events.eventText: Rachel Carson publishes Silent Spring
events.description: Launched the modern environmental movement

events.eventText: John F. Kennedy is assassinated in Dallas
events.description: Shot while riding in an open motorcade

events.eventText: China detonates its first nuclear weapon
events.description: Became the fifth nuclear-armed state

events.eventText: ARPANET sends its first message
events.description: The system crashed after transmitting just two letters: LO

events.eventText: Galen writes his medical treatises in Rome
events.description: His ideas went unchallenged for over 1,300 years

events.eventText: The Antonine Plague reaches Rome
events.description: May have killed 5 million people across the empire

events.eventText: Justinian publishes his Codex of Roman law
events.description: Became the foundation of most European legal systems

events.eventText: Crawford Long uses ether as a surgical anaesthetic
events.description: The patient felt nothing during tumour removal

events.eventText: John Snow traces cholera to a Soho water pump
events.description: Founded the science of epidemiology

events.eventText: Joseph Lister pioneers antiseptic surgery
events.description: Used carbolic acid to sterilise surgical instruments

events.eventText: Mata Hari is executed by firing squad in Paris
events.description: Whether she was actually a spy remains debated

events.eventText: Penicillin is first mass-produced for Allied troops
events.description: Saved countless lives on the D-Day battlefields

events.eventText: The Watergate break-in is discovered
events.description: Led to the only resignation of a US president

events.eventText: Edward Snowden leaks NSA surveillance documents
events.description: Revealed mass surveillance of millions of citizens

events.eventText: Aristarchus proposes the Sun is the centre
events.description: Correct about the heliocentric model, ignored for 1,800 years

events.eventText: Arkwright patents the water frame spinning machine
events.description: Kick-started factory-based manufacturing in Britain

events.eventText: Amundsen reaches the South Pole
events.description: Beat Robert Scott's team by 34 days

events.eventText: Einstein publishes general relativity
events.description: Predicted that gravity bends light — confirmed in 1919

events.eventText: Hillary and Tenzing summit Everest
events.description: News reached London on the morning of the coronation

events.eventText: Laika the dog is launched into orbit
events.description: First animal to orbit Earth, aboard Sputnik 2

events.eventText: Nirvana releases Nevermind
events.description: Knocked Michael Jackson off the top of the US charts

events.eventText: The first human genome sequence is published
events.description: Mapped roughly 3 billion base pairs of DNA

events.eventText: Voyager 1 enters interstellar space
events.description: Still transmitting data from over 24 billion km away

events.eventText: A fusion reactor produces net energy at the NIF
events.description: Achieved using 192 high-powered lasers

events.eventText: The Silk Road trade network flourishes
events.description: Stretched over 6,400 km from China to Rome

events.eventText: The Great Plague of London kills 68,000
events.description: Bodies collected nightly by carts

events.eventText: The Glorious Revolution deposes James II
events.description: William of Orange took the English throne without a battle

events.eventText: Karl Marx publishes Das Kapital Volume 1
events.description: Wrote most of it in the British Museum reading room

events.eventText: The first regular television broadcasts begin
events.description: The BBC launched from Alexandra Palace in London

events.eventText: Israel and Egypt sign the Camp David Accords
events.description: First peace treaty between Israel and an Arab state

events.eventText: Live Aid concerts raise money for Ethiopian famine
events.description: Broadcast to 1.9 billion people across 150 countries

events.eventText: Diego Maradona scores the Hand of God goal
events.description: Also scored the Goal of the Century in the same match

events.eventText: The Soviet Union formally dissolves
events.description: Gorbachev resigned on Christmas Day

events.eventText: Apple launches the iTunes Store
events.description: Sold one million songs in the first five days

events.eventText: Rice paddy farming begins in the Yangtze Delta
events.description: Now feeds more than half the world's population

events.eventText: Watermills appear across the Roman Empire
events.description: Automated the grinding of grain for the first time

events.eventText: Smallpox devastates the Aztec capital Tenochtitlan
events.description: Killed millions and enabled the Spanish conquest

events.eventText: Nathan Hale is hanged as an American spy
events.description: Reportedly said: I regret that I have but one life to give

events.eventText: Pierre de Coubertin founds the IOC
events.description: Revived the Olympic Games after a 1,500-year gap

events.eventText: Colossus, the first programmable computer, is built
events.description: Built at Bletchley Park to break German codes

events.eventText: The European Economic Community is founded
events.description: Six nations signed the Treaty of Rome

events.eventText: The International Criminal Court opens in The Hague
events.description: First permanent court for war crimes and genocide

events.eventText: The Indian Mars Orbiter Mission succeeds
events.description: India became the first country to reach Mars on its first attempt

events.eventText: Japan hosts the 2020 Tokyo Olympics (delayed to 2021)
events.description: Held without spectators due to the pandemic
